widgets_values:
Commonly adjusted parameters:
- Analyze Global Prompt Text: value 0.01-0.60
- Apply Advanced ControlNet: strength 0.4-0.7
- Apply Advanced ControlNet: end_percent 0.3+
- Scaled Soft Weights: base_multiplier 0.1+
Increasing strength too much reduces flexibility and makes the image look stiff.

## Custom Nodes Used

- ComfyUI-ppm
- rgthree-comfy
- ComfyUI_ADV_CLIP_emb
- cg-use-everywhere
- ComfyUI-Advanced-ControlNet
- comfyui-dynamicprompts

## Models Used

- ControlNet: CN-posetest_v2_1.safetensors

nodes.title: Yumil MPM Note
widgets_values:
## Requirements
- Extension: comfyui-yumil-mpm
- Application: Yumil MPM
- Reference image
- Region masks created from the reference image
---
## Before You Start

- If this is your first time using Yumil MPM, it is a good idea to create a sample project from the app and read at least the introductory tutorial first.
---
## Setup in Yumil MPM

1. In the CategoryTree, open the "PositivePrompt" category.
2. In the PromptTree, create a group named "Regional Prompt".
3. Add a new prompt named "template" inside the Regional Prompt group, then paste the template on the right.
4. While looking at the reference image, describe the pose and angle for each region.
5. Edit each `@@@_CategoryName_@@@` placeholder to match your own categories.
6. Add images to the PromptBrowser in this order: reference image, mask 1, mask 2, and so on.
7. To create more variations, duplicate the template from PromptTree > Selected:Add > Add Clone.

- The "Mask And Pose Tags Creator" workflow can be used to create masks and pose tags more easily.

---
## Notes on Prompt Creation

- LoRA files can be placed in any region.
- Trigger words must be placed in the specific region you want to target.
- Placing a character's trigger words in the global region can cause elements to mix, so use the global region carefully.
- In many cases, using the same pose and angle prompt for both Global and each Region works without problems. Since this can reduce setup effort, it may be worth trying first, then adjusting individual prompts only if the results are unsatisfactory.
- WD14 output tags can be useful as a reference when writing pose and angle prompts.
- Use Exclude Tags in WD14 output to automatically remove everything except pose and angle tags.
- For regional composition, some illustration models from more than a year ago may follow the intended layout more accurately than newer popular models. If the composition does not match the masks well, try experimenting with different models, including older ones.
- This workflow uses 2 regions, but it can be extended up to 5. If needed, duplicate the Region group and connect it to the Attention Couple node.
